preset_description: Dark = decision signal. Emphasis accumulates toward conversion — trust anchors and commitment moments are dark, top-of-funnel is light.

brief.audience: growth-focused SaaS teams who need a semantic design layer that adapts to visitor intent
brief.goal: demonstrate MODE's ability to generate intent-aware pages at funnel scale

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Hero (slim) provides essential page title and context — even on a conversion-stage page, the visitor needs a brief anchor confirming what they are about to commit to. Keeps it minimal per conversion-stage rules.

beats.name: Decision
beats.rationale: Conversion funnel demands the Decision beat immediately after the slim Hero. Presents plan comparison and pricing so the growth-focused SaaS team can make their final choice without friction.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: Placed immediately before Recovery per rules. Serves as the commitment prompt and action capture — the stripped conversion flow drives directly to sign-up or purchase.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Always-last beat providing secondary paths, footer links, and exit handling for visitors who aren't ready to convert on this visit.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation — wayfinding and persistent CTA for the session.

sections.name: Hero
sections.rationale: Hero (slim) provides essential page title and context — even on a conversion-stage page, the visitor needs a brief anchor confirming what they are about to commit to. Keeps it minimal per conversion-stage rules.

sections.name: Pricing
sections.rationale: Conversion funnel demands the Decision beat immediately after the slim Hero. Presents plan comparison and pricing so the growth-focused SaaS team can make their final choice without friction.

sections.name: CTA
sections.rationale: Placed immediately before Recovery per rules. Serves as the commitment prompt and action capture — the stripped conversion flow drives directly to sign-up or purchase.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: Page close — brand mark, minimal navigation, and legal.

page.reasoning: Conversion funnel stage requires minimal variant — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only. The notes state: 'minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only' and 'The persistent CTA in nav is the most converted element on most SaaS pages — never suppress it.'

Validator archetype on conversion stage requires social-proof variant per selection rules. The notes confirm: 'Validator variant always includes trust_signal' and 'with-social-proof variant places LogoStrip immediately below CTACluster,' providing the confidence signal Validators need at decision moment.

slots.eyebrow: Semantic design intelligence for SaaS teams
slots.headline: Your design system knows how to render. MODE teaches it why.
slots.subhead: One kit. Every visitor gets the component behavior, visual weight, and copy register their intent requires — resolved automatically.
slots.trust_signal: One-time purchase. No subscription. Yours to own, extend, and fork.

page.reasoning: Validator archetype on conversion stage benefits from the highlighted variant with full feature context. The notes state: 'Validator variant includes description and longer feature list' and 'highlighted variant for recommended plan' — this combination builds confidence through detailed, trustworthy presentation.

slots.description: The complete intelligence layer: semantic resolution engine, component manifest, palette map, IA planner, multi-variant generation, and Next.js preview UI. No subscription, no runtime dependency. Yours forever.
features: Semantic resolution engine — archetype, funnel stage, intent state, context mode
Component manifest — 11 components fully specified with slot schemas
IA planner + multi-variant generation via Claude Opus
Palette map with three out-of-the-box presets
Next.js preview UI with palette and label overlays
Bring your own Anthropic API key — no vendor billing

page.reasoning: Validator archetype requires trust signals in CTA. The notes state: 'Validator variant: include subhead and trust_signal (e.g., 'No credit card required')' — centered variant with subhead and trust_signal provides the reassurance Validators need before committing.

slots.headline: The semantic layer your design system is missing.
slots.subhead: Running in under an hour. Configured once. No lock-in, no recurring cost — just a design system that finally knows who it's talking to.
slots.trust_signal: One-time purchase · Lifetime access · No subscription required

page.reasoning: Conversion funnel stage on a key decision page requires recovery CTA. The notes state: 'with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows,' making this essential for capturing hesitant Validators.
